nodes.name: protocol.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the complete JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol layer for plugin SDK communication, including all method type definitions for host-to-worker and worker-to-host RPC calls, error codes, message serialization, and runtime type guards.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of discriminated union types and TypeScript literal types to model the full JSON-RPC message envelope without runtime overhead.

nodes.name: createRequest
nodes.summary: Constructs a typed JSON-RPC request message object with auto-incremented ID, method name, and parameters.

nodes.name: createErrorResponse
nodes.summary: Builds a JSON-RPC error response envelope with a structured error code, message, and optional data payload.

nodes.name: parseMessage
nodes.summary: Deserializes a newline-delimited JSON-RPC message string into a typed protocol message, throwing JsonRpcParseError on malformed input.

nodes.name: JsonRpcParseError
nodes.summary: Custom error class thrown when a raw message string cannot be parsed as a valid JSON-RPC protocol message.

nodes.name: JsonRpcCallError
nodes.summary: Error class representing a JSON-RPC call failure, carrying the server-side error code and optional structured data returned by the remote method.

nodes.name: testing.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a comprehensive in-process test harness for plugin SDK integrations, including fake environment drivers, assertion helpers for environment lifecycle events, and a full createTestHarness factory that simulates the host-worker RPC boundary.
nodes.languageNotes: The createTestHarness function spans nearly 2000 lines — it constructs an entire in-memory plugin host with full state management, making it the canonical integration test entry point.

nodes.name: createTestHarness
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a complete in-memory plugin host for integration testing, wiring together all host-side capabilities (access control, local folders, issues, routines, secrets) against a fake plugin worker.

nodes.name: createEnvironmentTestHarness
nodes.summary: Builds a test harness focused specifically on environment driver testing, providing lifecycle assertion helpers and a fake environment driver for unit tests.

nodes.name: createFakeEnvironmentDriver
nodes.summary: Creates a minimal in-memory environment driver stub that records lease operations and simulates execution responses, used for isolating plugin environment driver logic in tests.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the complete TypeScript type surface for the plugin SDK, covering plugin manifests, capability declarations (jobs, webhooks, tools, managed agents/projects/routines), and all host-to-worker and worker-to-host method parameter and result types.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports 80 named types with no runtime functions — a pure type definition module that models every capability and data contract in the plugin system.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the plugin SDK's UI layer, re-exporting all hooks, components, and utilities that plugin UIs use to interact with the Paperclip host environment from within a sandboxed iframe.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports 93 symbols from a single index — the canonical surface a plugin UI developer imports from.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines TypeScript types for the plugin launcher UI bridge, including render context snapshots, modal bounds, error codes, and close events used in host-iframe communication.

nodes.name: worker-rpc-host.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the runtime RPC host that bridges a plugin worker process to the Paperclip host over stdio, handling JSON-RPC message dispatch, capability enforcement, state management, and the full lifecycle of host-to-worker and worker-to-host method calls.
nodes.languageNotes: startWorkerRpcHost spans the entire file and is the core runtime of the plugin SDK — it manages process lifecycle, capability negotiation, and bidirectional RPC over stdin/stdout streams.

nodes.name: startWorkerRpcHost
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the complete plugin worker RPC host, establishing stdin/stdout transport, registering all method handlers, and managing plugin lifecycle from initialization through teardown.

nodes.name: runWorker
nodes.summary: Spawns a plugin worker as a child process with the correct entrypoint, environment variables, and IPC configuration for the RPC host to communicate with.

nodes.name: host-client-factory.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the plugin SDK host-client factory, verifying correct construction and wiring of host-side client objects that proxy calls to plugin workers.

nodes.name: testing-actions.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for plugin SDK testing action helpers, verifying that test harness action utilities behave correctly under expected inputs.

nodes.name: worker-rpc-host.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the worker RPC host, validating the full message round-trip between the host process and a plugin worker over stdio including error handling and capability enforcement.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the shared API prefix constant and API route namespace constants used consistently across both server and client packages.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Central repository of 136 named constants for the entire Paperclip platform, covering company, agent, issue, plugin, routine, secret, environment, and UI configuration values shared between server and client.
nodes.languageNotes: Also exports 4 small utility functions (clampIssueRequestDepth, pluginOperationIssueOriginKind, isPluginOperationIssueOriginKind, isSystemIssueDocumentKey) that are tightly coupled to constant value logic.

nodes.name: clampIssueRequestDepth
nodes.summary: Clamps an issue request depth value within the platform-defined min/max bounds, preventing overly deep or shallow issue graph traversals.

nodes.name: isPluginOperationIssueOriginKind
nodes.summary: Type guard that checks whether a given issue origin kind string belongs to the plugin operation origin family.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Primary barrel entry point for the shared package, re-exporting all 1121 symbols from constants, validators, types, project-mentions, and api sub-modules for unified consumption by server, client, and plugin packages.
nodes.languageNotes: With 1121 exports and zero functions or classes, this is a pure aggregation barrel — the single import surface for all shared Paperclip platform contracts.

nodes.name: project-mentions.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the project-mentions module, verifying href building and parsing round-trips for project, agent, user, skill, and routine mention types.

nodes.name: project-mentions.ts
nodes.summary: Implements URL-scheme-based mention href building and parsing for projects, agents, users, skills, and routines, plus ID extraction and normalization utilities used in rich-text editor mentions across the platform.

nodes.name: buildProjectMentionHref
nodes.summary: Constructs a custom URL-scheme href string for a project mention, encoding company and project identifiers.

nodes.name: parseProjectMentionHref
nodes.summary: Parses a project mention href back into structured company and project identifier components, returning null on invalid input.

nodes.name: extractProjectMentionIds
nodes.summary: Scans a rich-text document for all project mention hrefs and extracts the referenced project IDs into an array.

nodes.name: agent.ts
nodes.summary: Defines TypeScript types for agent entities in the Paperclip platform, including agent configuration, permissions, status, and runtime properties.

nodes.name: cloud-upstream.ts
nodes.summary: Defines types for cloud upstream integration, representing the data contracts for syncing local Paperclip instances with a hosted cloud backend.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel that re-exports all 409 type symbols from the shared types sub-modules (agent, issue, plugin, routine, secrets, workspace-runtime, etc.), providing the unified type namespace for the shared package.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure re-export barrel with 409 named exports and no runtime code.

nodes.name: instance.ts
nodes.summary: Defines instance-level configuration constants for Paperclip deployments, including backup retention presets and issue graph liveness recovery lookback bounds.

nodes.name: issue.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive type definitions for the Paperclip issue/task domain, covering issue status, priority, work modes, relations, comments, attachments, and the full issue graph data model.

nodes.name: plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the shared type contracts for the plugin system, including plugin database schemas, migration status, namespace modes, and the plugin runtime data model.

nodes.name: resource-memberships.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the RESOURCE_MEMBERSHIP_STATES constant and associated types governing access control membership lifecycle states across Paperclip resources.

nodes.name: routine.ts
nodes.summary: Defines TypeScript types for the routine (recurring scheduled task) domain, including routine configuration, schedule, status, and execution result types.

nodes.name: secrets.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the shared type contracts for the secrets management domain, covering secret providers, binding targets, access outcomes, versioning, and health status across Paperclip's secret infrastructure.

nodes.name: workspace-runtime.ts
nodes.summary: Defines types for workspace runtime configuration and state, covering environment provisioning, sandbox modes, and the runtime data model shared between the server and agent execution infrastructure.

nodes.name: agent.ts
nodes.summary: Exports Zod validation schemas for all agent-related API operations including create, update, permissions, instructions bundles, and runtime configuration, providing the shared request validation layer used by both server and client.
